questions.question_text: You are approaching a traffic light-controlled intersection and are required to continue straight ahead. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue in my own traffic lane up to the intersection and stop behind the bus.
answers.2: Of the two central traffic lanes, I move into the lane to the left of me and drive slowly up alongside the bus.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic lights.

questions.question_text: You have driven too far and want to turn as soon as possible. There are no road users behind you. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I make a U-turn immediately.
answers.2: I turn as soon as the oncoming driver has passed.
answers.3: I continue further ahead on the road and find another place to turn.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with the two slowly moving passenger cars with caravans. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I overtake both caravans at once.
answers.2: I overtake the nearest caravan and pull in in front of it.
answers.3: I reduce my speed.
answers.4: I pull over to the right of the traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You have reduced your speed in order to turn left at the intersection. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I turn left immediately.
answers.3: I apply my service brake.
answers.4: I wait until the oncoming traffic has passed.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 70 km/h. What must you be particularly mindful of in this situation?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of 70 km/h.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the risk of reduced traction.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the surroundings of the road.

questions.question_text: What is the highest sensible speed through this bend?

answers.1: 70 km/h.
answers.2: 50 km/h.
answers.3: 20 to 30 km/h.
answers.4: I am particularly mindful of the condition of the road surface.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 30 to 40 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue at an unchanged speed of approximately 30 to 40 km/h.
answers.2: I brake hard immediately.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the bicyclist ahead.
answers.4: I move further to the left in the traffic lane.

questions.question_text: You have moved out to the middle of the roadway in order to turn left. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I wait for any oncoming traffic.
answers.2: I turn left before the traffic island on the side road.
answers.3: I drive a little further forward.
answers.4: I turn left after the traffic island on the side road.

questions.question_text: Which speed rules must you be particularly mindful of here?

answers.1: The maximum speed limit of 50 km/h begins here.
answers.2: The speed limit of 50 km/h ends here.
answers.3: The maximum speed limit of 80 km/h begins here.

questions.question_text: You are driving on a road with one-way traffic and intend to continue straight ahead past the roadworks. What must you be particularly mindful of?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the lane markings.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of the merge rule.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the rule for changing traffic lanes.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with the slowly moving driver in front. How will you continue?

answers.1: I overtake.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I let the vehicle in front merge in ahead of me.
answers.4: I remain in my own traffic lane.

questions.question_text: What does the hand signal given by the police officer mean for you?

answers.1: I am permitted to drive straight ahead at the intersection.
answers.2: I have a duty to stop.
answers.3: I am permitted to turn left at the intersection.
answers.4: I am permitted to turn right at the intersection.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead. There are no road users behind you. How do you proceed?

answers.1: I pull over to the left side of the traffic lane immediately.
answers.2: I count on the oncoming drivers holding back for me.
answers.3: I remain on the right-hand side of the traffic lane all the way up to where the road narrows.
answers.4: If there is any oncoming traffic, I hold back.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 30 km/h, the same as the passenger car in front. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the shape of the road.
answers.2: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic ahead.

questions.question_text: You are about to catch up with the slowly moving passenger car in front, which is travelling at approximately 40 km/h. There are no road users behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I give a flashing light signal with my high-beam headlights.
answers.2: I overtake immediately.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of the traffic ahead.
answers.4: I brake and remain behind the traffic in front for the time being.

questions.question_text: You are in a hurry and have caught up with the passenger car in front. It is travelling at approximately 30 km/h. How will you continue?

answers.1: I turn my head and check the blind spots behind me and ahead of me.
answers.2: If there are no vehicles in the blind spots, I overtake immediately.
answers.3: I reduce my speed.
answers.4: I wait for a while before overtaking.

questions.question_text: You are required to continue straight ahead at the intersection. There are no road users behind you and the motorcyclist in front is slowing down. How will you continue?

answers.1: I overtake the motorcyclist.
answers.2: I am particularly mindful of children.
answers.3: I move my foot over to the brake pedal.
answers.4: I look to see what is happening on both sides before I drive into the intersection.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right as soon as possible. How do you proceed here?

answers.1: I stop at the stopline.
answers.2: When the lights turn green, I turn right here at the intersection.
answers.3: I am particularly mindful of bicyclists coming from behind on my right side.
answers.4: I wait before turning into the next side road.

questions.question_text: You intend to turn right at this junction. How will you proceed?

answers.1: I remain behind the white passenger car.
answers.2: When the white passenger car moves off, I follow it and turn right immediately.
answers.3: When the white passenger car moves off, I stop at the stopline.
answers.4: I hold back for traffic coming from the left before turning right.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 40 km/h and intend to continue straight ahead through this junction. How will you continue?

answers.1: I continue through the junction at an unchanged speed of approximately 40 km/h.
answers.2: I count on the passenger car from the right giving way.
answers.3: Priority to the right applies here.
answers.4: I look to see what is happening on both sides before I drive into the junction.

questions.question_text: You have reduced your speed in order to turn left at the intersection. There are no passenger cars or motorcyclists behind you. How will you continue?

answers.1: I warn the bicyclist with the horn.
answers.2: I count on the bicyclist holding back.
answers.3: I complete my left turn immediately in one large curve.
answers.4: I hold back for the bicyclist.

questions.question_text: You are about to enter a motorway, but the driver in front is hesitating about merging. There are no road users behind you in the nearest traffic lane on the motorway. How will you continue?

answers.1: I apply my service brake.
answers.2: I remain behind the drivers in front for the time being.
answers.3: I drive into the motorway across the broken line immediately.
answers.4: I continue on the access lane for a while longer.

questions.question_text: You are driving at approximately 60 km/h and have caught up with the passenger car on a long, steep uphill section. How will you continue?

answers.1: I am particularly mindful of the blind spots of the passenger car.
answers.2: I reduce my speed.
answers.3: I let the passenger car merge in ahead of me.
answers.4: I continue straight ahead on the right-hand side of the passenger car and merge in ahead of it.

questions.question_text: You have just stepped out of the passenger car. According to the legal provisions on stopping and parking, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.2: I am permitted to leave the passenger car here for loading or unloading.
answers.3: Parking is permitted here.

questions.question_text: You have stepped out of the red passenger car. According to the legal provisions, which of the following applies here?

answers.1: I am permitted to stop the passenger car here for 2 to 3 minutes.
answers.2: Parking is permitted here.
answers.3: Stopping is prohibited here.
